methods.name: enterRaces
methods.rawdescription:
Enter a race
throws a ScoreSmarterError exception if any conflict is detected.

Callers that want to surface conflicts to the user (boat-swap UX) should
call `findEntryConflicts` first and, if appropriate, `swapAndEnter`. This
method is a strict guard for any caller that doesn't.
methods.description:
Enter a race throws a ScoreSmarterError exception if any conflict is detected.

Callers that want to surface conflicts to the user (boat-swap UX) should call findEntryConflicts first and, if appropriate, swapAndEnter. This method is a strict guard for any caller that doesn't.

methods.name: findEntryConflicts
methods.rawdescription:
Find every existing race entry that prevents the proposed sign-on across
the selected races. Each conflict is reported with its reason (exact-hull
duplicate, helm-already-in-race for merged-helm series, hull-already-in-
race for merged-hull series) so the caller can present a swap dialog.

Joins each per-race competitor row to its SeriesEntry because identity
fields (helm/boatClass/sailNumber) live on the entry, not on the race
competitor.
methods.description:
Find every existing race entry that prevents the proposed sign-on across the selected races. Each conflict is reported with its reason (exact-hull duplicate, helm-already-in-race for merged-helm series, hull-already-in- race for merged-hull series) so the caller can present a swap dialog.

Joins each per-race competitor row to its SeriesEntry because identity fields (helm/boatClass/sailNumber) live on the entry, not on the race competitor.

methods.name: findOrCreateSeriesEntry
methods.rawdescription:
Find an existing per-hull SeriesEntry (matched by boatClass + sailNumber +
helm) or create one. Updates the entry's handicaps/personalHandicapBand
to the latest input so they stay current; identity fields are preserved.
methods.description: Find an existing per-hull SeriesEntry (matched by boatClass + sailNumber + helm) or create one. Updates the entry's handicaps/personalHandicapBand to the latest input so they stay current; identity fields are preserved.

methods.name: swapAndEnter
methods.rawdescription:
Boat-swap path: delete every conflicting race competitor (and any
SeriesEntry that becomes orphaned in *this* series as a result), then
proceed with the requested entry. The caller is expected to have already
obtained the user's consent via the conflict dialog.

The deletion is per-race: an existing entry that is also used in OTHER
races stays put and only loses its row in the conflicting race(s).
methods.description:
Boat-swap path: delete every conflicting race competitor (and any SeriesEntry that becomes orphaned in this series as a result), then proceed with the requested entry. The caller is expected to have already obtained the user's consent via the conflict dialog.

The deletion is per-race: an existing entry that is also used in OTHER races stays put and only loses its row in the conflicting race(s).

injectables.description:
Per-hull SeriesEntry creation.

Each unique (boatClass, sailNumber, helm) tuple in a series corresponds to exactly one SeriesEntry. The series-level entryAlgorithm only controls how those entries are merged at scoring time into competitor groups (see mergeKeyFor); it no longer affects how entries are created or how duplicate sign-on is detected. This guarantees per-hull data (handicap, boat class) is never overwritten when one helm sails multiple boats in the same series.
injectables.rawdescription:
Per-hull SeriesEntry creation.

Each unique (boatClass, sailNumber, helm) tuple in a series corresponds to
exactly one SeriesEntry. The series-level `entryAlgorithm` only controls
how those entries are *merged at scoring time* into competitor groups (see
`mergeKeyFor`); it no longer affects how entries are created or how
duplicate sign-on is detected. This guarantees per-hull data (handicap,
boat class) is never overwritten when one helm sails multiple boats in
the same series.

classes.description:
UI/scoring view of a RaceCompetitor combined with its SeriesEntry.

RaceCompetitor stores only scoring data and ids. All identity, boat, handicap and fleet metadata are read from the linked SeriesEntry.

Components that previously read flat fields off RaceCompetitor (helm, boatClass, sailNumber, handicaps, personalHandicapBand, fleetId, club) should consume ResolvedRaceCompetitor[] instead.

Identity rules:

  • helm, boatClass, sailNumber, handicaps, personalHandicapBand, fleetId, club come from the entry (per-hull, consistent across the series).
  • crew uses the per-race crewOverride when defined, otherwise the entry crew.
classes.rawdescription:
UI/scoring view of a RaceCompetitor combined with its SeriesEntry.

RaceCompetitor stores only scoring data and ids. All identity, boat,
handicap and fleet metadata are read from the linked SeriesEntry.

Components that previously read flat fields off RaceCompetitor (helm,
boatClass, sailNumber, handicaps, personalHandicapBand, fleetId, club)
should consume `ResolvedRaceCompetitor[]` instead.

Identity rules:
- `helm`, `boatClass`, `sailNumber`, `handicaps`, `personalHandicapBand`,
  `fleetId`, `club` come from the entry (per-hull, consistent across the
  series).
- `crew` uses the per-race `crewOverride` when defined, otherwise the
  entry crew.

components.name: AboutComponent
components.templateData:
ScoreSmarter analyses the results of orienteering events

It supports display of results from IOF XML version 2/3 files and various historical results file formats (SI and OE CVS formats, SI HTML files). ScoreSmarter has existed for many years in various forms. It was orignally written by Dave Ryder in Java and significantly enhanced by Ed Nash, Reinhard Balling and Andris Strazdins. It was ported to JavaScript by Luke Woodward (Github). This version has been ported to Typescript, includes some additional views and allows users to submit results onlineGithub Please mail support@splitsbrowser.org with any comment problems or issues. ScoreSmarter source code is located at Github å

components.name: PageComponent
components.template:
Pages in Storybook

We recommend building UIs with a component-driven process starting with atomic components and ending with pages.

Render pages with mock data. This makes it easy to build and review page states without needing to navigate to them in your app. Here are some handy patterns for managing page data in Storybook:

  • Use a higher-level connected component. Storybook helps you compose such data from the "args" of child component stories
  • Assemble data in the page component from your services. You can mock these services out using Storybook.

Get a guided tutorial on component-driven development at Storybook tutorials . Read more in the docs .

Tip Adjust the width of the canvas with the Viewports addon in the toolbar

import { Component } from '@angular/core'; import { CommonModule } from '@angular/common'; import { HeaderComponent } from './header.component'; import type { User } from './user'; @Component({ selector: 'storybook-page', standalone: true, imports: [CommonModule, HeaderComponent], template: `

Pages in Storybook

We recommend building UIs with a component-driven process starting with atomic components and ending with pages.

Render pages with mock data. This makes it easy to build and review page states without needing to navigate to them in your app. Here are some handy patterns for managing page data in Storybook:

  • Use a higher-level connected component. Storybook helps you compose such data from the "args" of child component stories
  • Assemble data in the page component from your services. You can mock these services out using Storybook.

Get a guided tutorial on component-driven development at Storybook tutorials . Read more in the docs .

Tip Adjust the width of the canvas with the Viewports addon in the toolbar
`, styleUrls: ['./page.css'], }) export class PageComponent { user: User | null = null; doLogout() { this.user = null; } doLogin() { this.user = { name: 'Jane Doe' }; } doCreateAccount() { this.user = { name: 'Jane Doe' }; } }

functions.name: aggregateCompetitorResults
functions.description:
Builds one IntermediateSeriesResult per distinct merge group. For each group we:

  • Iterate races in calendar (index) order.
  • Pick the first race in which any hull belonging to the group has a RaceResult; seed display fields from that hull's SeriesEntry.
  • For every race after the first appearance: append the group's points for that race, or a DNC where no hull in the group raced.

dncPoints for each group is mergeGroupCount + 1 where mergeGroupCount is the total number of distinct merge groups across the series.

functions.name: assignPointsForFinishers

Assigns points based on the competitor's ellapsed/corrected time. Competitors with the same value for the given key (e.g., correctedTime or rank) are awarded tied points.
When multiple competitors are tied, the score is rounded to 1 decimal place. Scoring penalty codes are included and the scoting penatly applied

Maps RaceCompetitor + linked SeriesEntry data to initial RaceResult objects.

All identity, boat and handicap fields are read from the SeriesEntry (the canonical per-hull source of truth). Crew is taken from comp.crewOverride ?? entry.crew. The competitorKey is derived from the series' merge strategy so series-level aggregation can collapse hulls when required (e.g. score-by-helm).

functions.name: computeManualPositionsForOrderEntry
functions.description: Computes target manualPosition for finishers in processed order (ties share the same value). Non-finishers (per isFinishedComp) get no rank (undefined).

functions.name: detectInRaceConflict
functions.description: Detects whether existing and incoming would clash inside the same race given the series' merging strategy. Returns null when the two entries can happily coexist in one race. The same-identity case always wins so callers can rely on the most specific reason being reported.

functions.name: determineOrdering
functions.description:
Determines the property to sort finishers and validates that all finishers have the required data. Throws ScoreSmarterError if complet data to rank all competitors is not avaliable Algorithm is:

  1. If Pursuit -> position.
  2. If Level Rating -> if manualPositions -> position, else elapsedTime.
  3. Handicap -> correctedTime.

functions.name: findCollidingEntry
functions.description: Returns the first entry in entries whose normalised per-hull identity matches identity, optionally ignoring an entry with id excludeId. The excludeId escape hatch is used when validating an in-place rename so the entry being edited doesn't match itself.

(Scoring pass eligibility / “has rating for this algorithm” is separate: use HandicapConfiguration.handicapScheme in the scoring engine.)

functions.name: isRaceScorable
functions.description:
Whether this race should be scored for the given fleet configuration.

ScoringEngine already restricts by race status (In progress, Completed, Published, Verified). For the primary GeneralHandicap fleet, that is enough: the race only becomes In progress once someone has a real result, and Completed means no row is still NOT_FINISHED.

With secondary scoring (separate configs per fleet), the same race can be In progress because another fleet has results while every competitor in this fleet is still NOT_FINISHED. Race status does not distinguish fleets, so we still require at least one non–NOT FINISHED row in filtered for non-GeneralHandicap fleets.

Resolves the Handicap[] for a given series using:

  1. source overrides,
  2. club class defaults,
  3. per-scheme metadata defaults.

functions.name: resolveRaceCompetitors

functions.name: score
functions.description:
Orchestrates the entire scoring process for a series as a pure function. It implements the "Scoring Grid" pattern for multi-pass calculations.

  1. Initializes a mutable "scoring grid".
  2. Scores the current race (if provided) and updates the grid.
  3. Performs series scoring (handles discards and RDG).
  4. Applies series-dependent scores (like averages) back to the grid.

seriesCompetitorCount is the count of distinct merge groups in the series (NOT the count of per-hull entries) and must be supplied by the caller. mergeStrategy controls how per-hull entries are collapsed into competitors at series-aggregation time (see mergeKeyFor).

jsdoctags.comment: An object containing the final scored races and series results.

functions.name: scoreRace
functions.description:
Uses competitor start, finish. lap and status to calculate results for a single race. All data in the ResultsData object is populated (elapsed/corrected times points and positions).

Assumes all results for the race are supplied.
The maximum number of laps and number of starters is calculated based on all results.

functions.name: scoreSeries
functions.description:
Aggregates pre-scored race results into a final, ranked series result.

Per-hull RaceResults are grouped by competitorKey so that merging strategies (e.g. "score by helm") collapse multiple hulls into a single series row. Display fields (helm, boatClass, sailNumber, handicap, PHB, club, crew) are seeded from the per-hull SeriesEntry corresponding to the first chronological race contribution for each merge group; race-level tables continue to show the actual per-race details.

functions.name: segmentProcessedPlacedAndNonPlaced
functions.description: Split processed queue into placed finishers vs penalties / DNF (order preserved).

functions.name: seriesOverlapsLocalDay
functions.description: True when the series' published date span includes the given calendar day (local timezone). Used to avoid fetching races for series that cannot possibly contain today's races.

functions.name: seriesOverlapsRecentLocalDays
functions.description: True when the series date span intersects the last N local calendar days.

functions.name: sortByFinishingOrder
functions.description: Sorts results by a specified ordering property, ensuring that finishers always appear before non-finishers.

functions.name: sortByPoints
functions.description: Sorts by points. Any boat that does not have any points yet assigned is sorted to the bottom.

functions.name: sortEntries
functions.description: Sort entries by boat class and sail number

functions.name: sortResolvedCompetitors
functions.description: Sorts resolved competitors by boatClass then sailNumber.

functions.name: toDbModel
functions.description:
Recursively converts properties of an object from App-space to DB-space.

  • undefined values are converted to null for full writes. For partial writes, they are skipped as Firestore does not support undefined.
  • Date objects are converted to Timestamp objects.
  • Nested objects and arrays are recursively converted.

functions.name: toFirebaseId
functions.description: Removes any invalid character from a string so it can be used as a Firestore object Id

functions.name: toSailwaveCode
functions.description: Sailwave uses capitalised results codes

functions.name: uniqueSeriesCandidatesForDay
functions.description: Dedupe by series id while preserving a stable order (first occurrence wins).

functions.name: uniqueSeriesCandidatesForRecentDays
functions.description: Dedupe by series id while preserving stable order for recent-day views.

functions.name: updateGridWithRace
functions.description: Updates the race grid with results from a scored race either add a new race or replacing an exisit on with updated scores

functions.name: validateFinishersHaveData
functions.description: Checks that all finishers have a valid (non-zero) value for the specified ordering property. Throws a SailbrowserError if any finisher is missing data.

typealiases.name: CompetitorKey
typealiases.description:
Identifier used to group SeriesEntry rows into a single competitor for series-level aggregation.

Per-hull SeriesEntries are always created at sign-on (one per boatClass+sailNumber+helm). The series-level entryAlgorithm controls only how those per-hull entries are merged when scoring the series:

  • classSailNumberHelm / regatta -> no merge (key = entry.id)
  • classSailNumber -> merge boats sailed by different helms in the same hull
  • helm -> merge entries that share a helm

mergeKeyFor deliberately uses normalised (trimmed, case-insensitive) helm/class strings so trivial typo variants don't split a competitor.

typealiases.name: CrewEditScope
typealiases.description: Per-race overrides are now intentionally minimal. Only crew may differ between races for the same hull (handled via RaceCompetitor.crewOverride). Every other piece of identity / boat / handicap data lives on the SeriesEntry and is therefore inherently series-wide.

typealiases.name: EditOperation
typealiases.rawtype: literal type | literal type | literal type | literal type | literal type | literal type | literal type

typealiases.name: EntryConflictReason
typealiases.description:
Why a proposed entry conflicts with an existing one in the same race:

  • sameEntry — exact (class+sail+helm) match. Always a conflict regardless of strategy: the same hull is being signed on twice.
  • sameHullDifferentHelm — strategy classSailNumber only. Two helms on the same physical boat in one race.
  • sameHelmDifferentHull — strategy helm only. The same sailor signed onto two boats in one race (impossible on the water and breaks merged-helm scoring).

typealiases.name: HandicapScheme
typealiases.description: Handicap system used to hamdicap between different boat types

typealiases.name: SeriesEntryMatchingStrategy
typealiases.description: How race entries are grouped to form series entries. For a 'regatta' all entrants will be considered to the series entrants For club racing, Series results will be derived from race entrants based on combinations of class/sail number/helm.

enumerations.name: ResultCodeAlgorithm
enumerations.description: The algorthim to use in calculating the points for a code.

<T>(): FirestoreDataConverter<T> => ({
   toFirestore(data: PartialWithFieldValue<T>, options?: SetOptions): DocumentData {
      const partialObject = options !== undefined;
      return toDbModel(data, partialObject, true);
   },
   fromFirestore: (snap: QueryDocumentSnapshot): T => {

      return { ...toAppModel<T>(data), id: snap.id };

})

src/app/scoring/services/series-scorer.t.name: aggregateCompetitorResults
src/app/scoring/services/series-scorer.t.description:
Builds one IntermediateSeriesResult per distinct merge group. For each group we:

  • Iterate races in calendar (index) order.
  • Pick the first race in which any hull belonging to the group has a RaceResult; seed display fields from that hull's SeriesEntry.
  • For every race after the first appearance: append the group's points for that race, or a DNC where no hull in the group raced.

dncPoints for each group is mergeGroupCount + 1 where mergeGroupCount is the total number of distinct merge groups across the series.

src/app/scoring/services/series-scorer.t.name: applyClubOod

src/app/scoring/services/series-scorer.t.name: applyIsafRedress
src/app/scoring/services/series-scorer.t.description: Sets the points for ISAF Redress codes (RDGA, RDGB)

src/app/scoring/services/series-scorer.t.name: scoreSeries
src/app/scoring/services/series-scorer.t.description:
Aggregates pre-scored race results into a final, ranked series result.

Per-hull RaceResults are grouped by competitorKey so that merging strategies (e.g. "score by helm") collapse multiple hulls into a single series row. Display fields (helm, boatClass, sailNumber, handicap, PHB, club, crew) are seeded from the per-hull SeriesEntry corresponding to the first chronological race contribution for each merge group; race-level tables continue to show the actual per-race details.

src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.name: applyStaticRacePenalties
src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.description: Applies panalties that are not dependent on other results in the series.

src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.name: assignPointsForFinishers
src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.description:
Assigns points based on the competitor's ellapsed/corrected time. Competitors with the same value for the given key (e.g., correctedTime or rank) are awarded tied points.
When multiple competitors are tied, the score is rounded to 1 decimal place. Scoring penalty codes are included and the scoting penatly applied

src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.name: buildRaceResults
src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.description:
Maps RaceCompetitor + linked SeriesEntry data to initial RaceResult objects.

All identity, boat and handicap fields are read from the SeriesEntry (the canonical per-hull source of truth). Crew is taken from comp.crewOverride ?? entry.crew. The competitorKey is derived from the series' merge strategy so series-level aggregation can collapse hulls when required (e.g. score-by-helm).

src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.name: calculateRacePoints
src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.description: Recalculates points and ranks for a race that already has times calculated. This is useful when the number of competitors in the series changes, affecting penalty points (SCP, ZFP, etc.).

src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.name: calculateRanks
src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.description: Calculates competitor ranks within the race. Competitors tied on points will recieve the same rank.

src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.name: calculateTimes
src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.description: Calculates elapsed and corrected times for each result.

src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.name: determineOrdering
src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.description:
Determines the property to sort finishers and validates that all finishers have the required data. Throws ScoreSmarterError if complet data to rank all competitors is not avaliable Algorithm is:

  1. If Pursuit -> position.
  2. If Level Rating -> if manualPositions -> position, else elapsedTime.
  3. Handicap -> correctedTime.

src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.name: scoreRace
src/app/scoring/services/race-scorer.ts.description:
Uses competitor start, finish. lap and status to calculate results for a single race. All data in the ResultsData object is populated (elapsed/corrected times points and positions).

Assumes all results for the race are supplied.
The maximum number of laps and number of starters is calculated based on all results.

src/app/shared/firebase/firestore-helper.name: toDbModel
src/app/shared/firebase/firestore-helper.description:
Recursively converts properties of an object from App-space to DB-space.

  • undefined values are converted to null for full writes. For partial writes, they are skipped as Firestore does not support undefined.
  • Date objects are converted to Timestamp objects.
  • Nested objects and arrays are recursively converted.

src/app/published-results/services/scori.name: competitorsForConfigRace
src/app/published-results/services/scori.description:
Returns Series entries to include in the series for a given ScoringConfiguration. To be incliuded

  1. The entry is in the fleet (handicap range, BoatClass, tag)
  2. The entry has a handicap value for the handicap scheme (if required)

src/app/published-results/services/scori.name: isRaceScorable
src/app/published-results/services/scori.description:
Whether this race should be scored for the given fleet configuration.

ScoringEngine already restricts by race status (In progress, Completed, Published, Verified). For the primary GeneralHandicap fleet, that is enough: the race only becomes In progress once someone has a real result, and Completed means no row is still NOT_FINISHED.

With secondary scoring (separate configs per fleet), the same race can be In progress because another fleet has results while every competitor in this fleet is still NOT_FINISHED. Race status does not distinguish fleets, so we still require at least one non–NOT FINISHED row in filtered for non-GeneralHandicap fleets.

src/app/results-input/services/series-en.name: detectInRaceConflict
src/app/results-input/services/series-en.description: Detects whether existing and incoming would clash inside the same race given the series' merging strategy. Returns null when the two entries can happily coexist in one race. The same-identity case always wins so callers can rely on the most specific reason being reported.

src/app/results-input/services/series-en.name: findAllMatchingEntries
src/app/results-input/services/series-en.description: Returns ALL entries that share identity. Callers should normally see at most one match; more than one indicates a corrupt dataset that violates the per-hull invariant and must be resolved before further writes.

src/app/results-input/services/series-en.name: findCollidingEntry
src/app/results-input/services/series-en.description: Returns the first entry in entries whose normalised per-hull identity matches identity, optionally ignoring an entry with id excludeId. The excludeId escape hatch is used when validating an in-place rename so the entry being edited doesn't match itself.

src/app/scoring/services/scorer.ts.name: score
src/app/scoring/services/scorer.ts.description:
Orchestrates the entire scoring process for a series as a pure function. It implements the "Scoring Grid" pattern for multi-pass calculations.

  1. Initializes a mutable "scoring grid".
  2. Scores the current race (if provided) and updates the grid.
  3. Performs series scoring (handles discards and RDG).
  4. Applies series-dependent scores (like averages) back to the grid.

seriesCompetitorCount is the count of distinct merge groups in the series (NOT the count of per-hull entries) and must be supplied by the caller. mergeStrategy controls how per-hull entries are collapsed into competitors at series-aggregation time (see mergeKeyFor).

jsdoctags.comment: An object containing the final scored races and series results.

src/app/scoring/services/merge-key.ts.name: CompetitorKey
src/app/scoring/services/merge-key.ts.description:
Identifier used to group SeriesEntry rows into a single competitor for series-level aggregation.

Per-hull SeriesEntries are always created at sign-on (one per boatClass+sailNumber+helm). The series-level entryAlgorithm controls only how those per-hull entries are merged when scoring the series:

  • classSailNumberHelm / regatta -> no merge (key = entry.id)
  • classSailNumber -> merge boats sailed by different helms in the same hull
  • helm -> merge entries that share a helm

mergeKeyFor deliberately uses normalised (trimmed, case-insensitive) helm/class strings so trivial typo variants don't split a competitor.

Every other piece of identity / boat / handicap data lives on the SeriesEntry and is therefore inherently series-wide.

src/app/results-input/services/series-en.name: EntryConflictReason
src/app/results-input/services/series-en.description:
Why a proposed entry conflicts with an existing one in the same race:

  • sameEntry — exact (class+sail+helm) match. Always a conflict regardless of strategy: the same hull is being signed on twice.
  • sameHullDifferentHelm — strategy classSailNumber only. Two helms on the same physical boat in one race.
  • sameHelmDifferentHull — strategy helm only. The same sailor signed onto two boats in one race (impossible on the water and breaks merged-helm scoring).
